fix: replace echo with printf for binfmt registration (shellcheck SC2028)

which is the correct behavior for binfmt_misc magic byte registration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add aggressive anti-inflation scoring rules to prompts

The AI model (MiniMax M2.7) was giving 9s and 10s too freely.
This adds strict anti-inflation guardrails to both the React
defaults and the edge function fallback prompts:

- Separate 9 (top 10%) and 10 (perfect, ~1% of scores) with
  explicit hard gates for each
- Expected score distribution: 1-3 (~20%), 4-6 (~55%), 7-8 (~20%),
  9 (~4%), 10 (~1%)
- Hard gates: 7+ requires specific transcript citation, 8+ requires
  two specific behaviours AND zero major failures, 10 requires zero
  identifiable improvements
- Common inflation traps: don't give 7+ on Discovery for long calls,
  don't conflate owner engagement with rep quality, etc.
- Final anti-inflation checkpoint: if 5+ dimensions are 8+, re-read
  justifications; any 10 must survive "can I name one improvement?" test
- Updated all 12 dimension score guidance sections to split 9-10 into
  separate 9 and 10 tiers with stricter criteria

ci: publish multi-tag GitHub releases on tag (major, major.minor, major.minor.build, latest, stable)

Pre-releases (rc) only publish the exact tag.

commit.message:
feat: implement two-dimensional group architecture with 19 scopes and enhanced access control

Refactors authentication and authorization to use a two-dimensional group architecture separating UI view (type groups: t-admin, t-user) from resource access control (resource groups: g-admins-*, g-users-*). Adds tagging and costs scopes, enforces demo-admin write restrictions, implements default page routing based on scopes, and updates all documentation.

Key Changes:
- Two-dimensional groups: Type groups (t-admin, t-user) for UI layout, Resource groups (g-admins-*, g-users-*) for access control
- 19 scopes: Added tagging:read/write for tag policy management and costs:read/write for cost dashboard
- Scope assignments: g-admins-super has all scopes, g-admins-demo has read-only + demo write + costs:write
- Demo-admin restrictions: Can only create/delete resources in demo group (enforced in backend)
- Resource filtering: Admins see all including untagged, users filtered by loom:group tag
- Default page routing: App.tsx determines first accessible page based on user's scopes
- Tag policy scopes: Changed from settings:read/write to tagging:read/write
- Integration display: Added memory, MCP, and A2A badge display on agent cards
- Remove delete buttons for resources outside user's group (demo-admin)
- Remove traces section from invocation detail page
- Updated all SPECIFICATIONS.md and README.md with latest design decisions

Backend:
- Updated GROUP_SCOPES mapping in auth.py with two-dimensional structure
- Added group validation in agents.py and memories.py for demo-admin
- Changed costs.py endpoints to use costs:read/write scopes
- Changed settings.py tag endpoints to use tagging:read/write scopes
- Updated invocations.py invoke permission check for new group structure
- Fixed tag policy list endpoints to require only authentication (for card display)
- Enhanced AgentResponse with memory_names, mcp_names, a2a_names fields
- Updated test_scopes.py and test_costs.py with new group names

Frontend:
- Updated GROUP_SCOPES in AuthContext.tsx to match backend
- Updated App.tsx with getDefaultPersona() for scope-based routing
- Added userGroups prop to cards and pages for group-based restrictions
- Updated AgentDetailPage.tsx with correct two-dimensional invoke permission logic
- Removed "None" option from tag profile selector in ResourceTagFields.tsx
- Added integration badges (memory, MCP, A2A) to AgentCard.tsx
- Added delete restrictions based on group ownership in AgentCard.tsx and MemoryCard.tsx
- Added edit/delete restrictions on TaggingPage.tsx for non-super-admins
- Removed traces section from InvocationDetailPage.tsx

Infrastructure:
- Added tagging:read/write and costs:read/write to Cognito resource server scopes
- Created type groups: TypeAdminGroup (t-admin), TypeUserGroup (t-user)
- Renamed admin groups to g-admins-* format (super, demo, security, memory, mcp, a2a)
- Created user groups: g-users-demo, g-users-test, g-users-strategics
- Updated all user-to-group assignments to include both type and resource groups

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove redundant dueSoonWindowDays filter from PersonStatusService (#269)

The dueSoonPeople() method applied a second filter (days <= settings.dueSoonWindowDays)
after FrequencyCalculator.status() already determined .dueSoon via cadence.warningDays.
This caused contacts to be invisible on Home View when warningDays > dueSoonWindowDays
(e.g., Monthly cadence: warningDays=5, dueSoonWindowDays=3 → contacts 4-5 days from
due were excluded). Now FrequencyCalculator is the single source of truth for Due Soon.

Closes #268

Co-authored-by: Brad Slavin <brad@Brads-MacBook-Air.local>

fix: align scoring system to 4-dimension composite formula

- Update test suite: expects 4 dimensions (30/25/25/20) instead of 3
  (30/30/40). All 29 tests now pass.
- Fix cold-calls-sync: replace unweighted average of 5 wrong columns
  with proper v4 weighted formula using the correct 4 composite
  dimensions (opener_tone, call_structure, discovery_quality,
  closing_next_step). Missing dimensions redistribute weight
  proportionally.
- Update SCORE_DIMENSIONS comments: document 4-dimension composite
  formula with DB column mapping and separate composite sub-dimensions
  from standalone coaching dimensions.

commit.message:
Add multi-format film support: Super 8, 8mm Regular, Super 16

New film_format parameter controls perforation detection strategy:
- super8: 1 perf, left ROI (existing behavior, now default)
- 8mm: 2 perfs per frame, right ROI — midpoint between both used as anchor
- super16: 1 perf, right ROI

_best_contour() now accepts top_n to return the N best candidates.
detect_perforation() handles ROI side selection and dual-perf midpoint.
UI shows a format selector (both Tkinter and Electron) above the params.
CLI adds --film-format argument; film_format appears in the report.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: close final 5 parity gaps — zero skipped tests remaining

Ore germination: now rejects bridge cells (template check) and
building-occupied cells during ore spread, matching C++ cell.cpp:
3000,3007-3008.

PLAYER_ENTERED: trigger event now checks entering unit's house
against event.data, matching C++ tevent.cpp:290-291.

SET/CLEAR_GLOBAL: only reports globalChanged when value actually
changed, matching C++ scenario.cpp:268 idempotency check.

All 27,762 tests pass. Zero failures. Zero skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tailgate detection flyout and fix SubAddr-based direction logic

agent.ps1:
- Who's In Now: uses SubAddr from Net2Events (not unreliable CurrentAreaID)
- Staff entrance ACU 8129015 has reversed wiring: SubAddr=2=In, SubAddr=1=Out
- Events now include isIn field for direction (used by anomaly detection)
- first_last SQL: removed expensive anomalies CTE self-join that caused
  Net2DataAgent to hang 10+ min on YTD data
- anomalyCount (consecutive same-direction pairs) now computed in PowerShell
  from already-fetched events data; CommandTimeout raised to 60 for first_last
- Invoke-Query accepts optional timeout parameter

net2/index.html:
- First/Last tab: direction pills (In/Out) on first and last swipe of each day
- All alert pills (tailgates, no-entry swipe, no-exit swipe) are now clickable
- Tailgate detail flyout: slide-in right panel shows annotated swipe timeline
  with consecutive same-direction pairs highlighted and bridge annotations
  explaining the missing swipe (In→In or Out→Out sequences)
- No-Swipe Alerts: anomaly table alert pills also open the flyout
- noEntryLink / noExitLink / tailgateLink helpers for safe onclick generation

commit.message:
feat: add Report 01 — SOC prototype (Rmd + data prep script)

reports/report_01_soc_ossl_prototype/
  - report_01_soc_prototype.Rmd   : full professional Rmd report
    * Data description: OSSL v1.2, ASD sensor filter, sample structure,
      replicate handling (grouped CV by layer UUID, Kaufman & Rosset 2012)
    * SOC distribution: raw and log₁p histograms with skewness
    * Spectra visualisation: raw reflectance + all 3 preprocessing treatments
      coloured by SOC (viridis plasma scale)
    * Full CV metrics tables for log and no-log modes (mean ± SD, 30 resamples)
    * Best-model metric boxes with visual highlight (green = winner)
    * Obs vs pred panels loaded from pre-generated PNGs
    * Log vs no-log comparison bar chart
    * MSD decomposition table (SB, NU, LC)
    * Best model summary table
    * Conclusions + complete References section
    * Self-contained HTML (flatly theme, custom CSS, author badges)
  - prepare_report_data.R         : extracts 1000-sample modelling dataset,
    builds preprocessing stack, saves compact report_data.rds (25 MB)
    for fast Rmd loading (avoids re-reading 174 MB OSSL CSV.gz at knit time)

.gitignore: exclude reports/**/*.html, *.rds, *_cache/, *_files/

Add remaining commands (matches, feed, photos, music, videos, calendar, seeking, musiclist, hidden, settings)

Implement all 10 remaining command modules with full CLI subcommands:
- matches: list (type=new/new-members, --page)
- feed: list (--filter, --limit), like, unlike
- photos: list, upload, set-main, delete, reorder
- music: list, upload, delete, master, master-status, download-master
- videos: list, add, delete, reorder
- calendar: list, add, delete
- seeking: get, set (--join-band, --instruments)
- musiclist: list, add, remove
- hidden: list, add, remove
- settings: email get/set, matchmailer get/set, dashboard get/set, password update

Also adds parsers for matchmailer, dashboard, musiclist, and hidden to parser.py.
Wires all commands into main.py. Bumps version to 0.8.0.
Includes comprehensive tests for all parsers and CLI commands.

commit.message:
feat: SCG11EA WEAP + replacement tanks + 2-cell chain spacing

- Added WEAP to build order (3rd building) — big chain link + tank factory
- Tank production capped at 5 — build replacements to defend chain
- 2-cell chain spacing (was 3) — POWR sight=3 only reveals 3 cells
  from edge, so next building at +3 has fog-of-war on its far cell
- 14 POWRs in build order to span the 40-cell east chain
- coreProductionReady simplified to use hasWarFactory (now in build order)

Results: max structs=74, chain reaches x=52+, army sustains with
tank replacements. SYRD not yet reached — chain needs more time
or the east stretch needs fewer obstacles.

- Import all 46 MD3 tokens from design HTML as base layer
- Rename secondary→cultural-bronze, success→cultural-sage,
  warning→cultural-amber, error→cultural-coral
- MD3 secondary (#465f89), tertiary (#964400), error (#ba1a1a) now canonical

## Fixes
- on-surface: #1c1c19 → #181c22 (match design HTML)
- outline: #7a7770 → #717785 (match design HTML)
- outline-variant: #c9c5be → #c1c6d5 (match design HTML)
- #1275E2 → #005ab4 (4 CSS gradients, old Ethos primary)
- #FAF7F2 → #f9f9ff (13 components, old Ethos background)
- #334155 → #414753 (2 places in Vulca3DBackground)
- pulseGlow/ios-focus rgba from old to new primary

## Scope
29 files, +434/-395 lines | Build pass, 0 TS errors, 0 ESLint errors
